zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Elizabeth Warrene於1429年出生在英國爾波易頓時，她的父親John Warren III15歲，母親Isabel Stanley23歲。她於大約1452年與Robert Rockley在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1508年在英國越可爾利德斯享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Elizabeth Warrene于1429年出生在英国爾波易頓时，她的父亲John Warren III15岁，母亲Isabel Stanley23岁。她于大约1452年与Robert Rockley在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1508年在英国越可爾利德斯享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Warrene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Warren
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Elizabeth Warrene was born in 1429, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, her father, John Warren III, was 15 and her mother, Isabel Stanley, was 23. She married Robert Rockley about 1452, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1508, in Leeds, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Elizabeth Warrene est née en 1429, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John Warren III, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Isabel Stanley, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Rockley environ 1452, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1508, à Leeds, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Elizabeth Warrene 1429 in Poynton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Warren III 15 und ihre Mutter Isabel Stanley 23. Sie hat um 1452 in Yorkshire, England Robert Rockley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1508 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Leeds, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Elizabeth Warrene è nata il nel 1429 nel Poynton, Cheshire, England, sua padre, John Warren III, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Isabel Stanley, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Robert Rockley circa 1452, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1508, nel Leeds, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Elizabeth Warreneが1429年にイギリスチェシュアイアポイントンで生まれた時，父John Warren IIIは15歳，母Isabel Stanleyは23歳であった。約1452年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでRobert Rockleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1508年にイギリスヨークシュアイアリーズで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Elizabeth Warrene(이)가 1429년 영국 체실르 포이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Warren III(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Stanley(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1452년 영국 요르크실르에서 Robert Rockley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1508년 영국 요르크실르 리드즈에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Elizabeth Warrene nasceu em 1429, em Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Warren III, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Stanley, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Rockley aproximadamente 1452, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1508, em Leeds, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Elizabeth Warrene родилась в 1429 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Warren III было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Stanley, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Rockley около 1452 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1508 г. в местности лидс, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Elizabeth Warrene nació en 1429, en Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Warren III, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Isabel Stanley, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Robert Rockley aproximadamente 1452, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1508, en Leeds, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años.
